title: Public Records Request: AI Software Procurement and Policies (School of Biomedical Sciences and Healthcare Administration)

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request: AI Software Procurement and Policies (School of Biomedical Sciences and Healthcare Administration)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any records created, received, or maintained by the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee, specifically as they pertain to the School of Biomedical Sciences and Healthcare Administration:

Contracts and Licensing: All active contracts, service-level agreements (SLAs), and licensing agreements between the University (or the School specifically) and third-party vendors for AI-related software or services. This includes tools for generative AI and AI-detection services (e.g., Turnitin’s AI writing indicator, GPTZero, Copyleaks, or similar).

Deliberative Documents and Minutes: All public minutes, agendas, or supplemental documents from School-level faculty meetings, curriculum committees, or administrative leadership meetings where the usage, procurement, or ethical implications of third-party AI processing/detection software were discussed.

Official Policies: All official policies, handbooks, or "best practice" memoranda issued by the School of Biomedical Sciences and Healthcare Administration governing how faculty may or must use third-party vendors to detect AI in student work.

Syllabi Requirements: Any standardized syllabi language or templates mandated or recommended by the School regarding the submission of student work to third-party AI detection platforms.

Privacy and Security: Any Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) or FERPA-compliance reviews specific to the School’s use of these vendors, particularly regarding the storage and processing of student-generated data.

Accuracy and Efficacy: Any internal reports or assessments regarding the accuracy, "false positive" rates, or ethical reviews of AI detection tools currently utilized within this School.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Wisconsin Resident
